title: Central Dogmas Self Destruct System Or Lack Thereof
display_title: Central Dogmas Self Destruct System Or Lack Thereof
wikitext:
[]: Central Dogma's Self-Destruct System (Or Lack Thereof)             Quote Post by monitoradiation » Sun Sep 29, 2013 3:45 pm [Split from here . - Monk] Kendrix wrote: Funny, I don't remember Ritsuko saying anything about an actual tranfer or other alternatives, just "you need to protect Lillith and EVA 01 first and foremost!" Did they even have TIME to transfer Lillith? wouldn't the angel just change course? Whatever location they could've taken Lillith to, it would probably be less armored and easier to destroy than the geofront.

[]:             Quote Post by Dr. Talos » Sun Sep 29, 2013 3:56 pm monitoradiation wrote: I actually wonder at times whether or not Ritsuko or Misato had thought of self-destructing Terminal Dogma - As big Z is going down there, that would seem to me the only option left had Shinji not came through. And they would've had to do it before Big Z came into the command center or else everyone dies and nobody is left to activate it. That they didn't even mention that option tells me that they don't have a manual detonation.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun Sep 29, 2013 4:03 pm monitoradiation wrote: I actually wonder at times whether or not Ritsuko or Misato had thought of self-destructing Terminal Dogma - As big Z is going down there, that would seem to me the only option left had Shinji not came through. And they would've had to do it before Big Z came into the command center or else everyone dies and nobody is left to activate it. That they didn't even mention that option tells me that they don't have a manual detonation. You would think that someone would figure out that keeping an angel in your basement when it can trigger 3I, and have an auto-detonate but no failsafe maual detonate is about just as dumb as a sack of rocks unless someone higher up needs it for some other purpose...

[]:             Quote Post by monitoradiation » Sun Sep 29, 2013 4:10 pm Dr. Talos wrote: They do have a manual self destruct. As Kaworu is about to penetrate Terminal Dogma Misato and Hyuga prepare to initiate the self destruct for the entire facility in the event of Unit 1's signal being lost. The self destruct is also nearly initiated when Ireul takes over the Magi in episode 13.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun Sep 29, 2013 4:26 pm monitoradiation wrote: It ought to ring bells similar to how in the manga (not canon, I know; but I can't remember atm if NGE Misato thought the same thing as in the manga) that when eva-00 went down to retrieve LoL that she started suspecting that something is up.

[]:             Quote Post by Dr. Talos » Sun Sep 29, 2013 4:54 pm ElMariachi wrote: That was in NGE, in Rebuild Misato explicitly said Zeruel hiding his signature behind EVA-00 means that CD won't self-destruct, meaning that they don't have any manual way to do it.

[]:             Quote Post by Giji Shinka » Sun Sep 29, 2013 5:58 pm Dr. Talos wrote: Ah yes, my bad. Seems a pretty big design flaw to not have the ability to manually self destruct if everything goes to hell. You'd think a self destruct system is something you would not want to leave only to an automated system.

[]:             Quote Post by Dr. Talos » Sun Sep 29, 2013 6:11 pm Giji Shinka wrote: Mmmmmm....Did they mention that it was automated only program? Maybe it had manual one.

[]:             Quote Post by monitoradiation » Sun Sep 29, 2013 6:19 pm Giji Shinka wrote: Mmmmmm....Did they mention that it was automated only program? Maybe it had manual one.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun Sep 29, 2013 8:06 pm monitoradiation wrote: I actually wonder at times whether or not Ritsuko or Misato had thought of self-destructing Terminal Dogma - As big Z is going down there, that would seem to me the only option left had Shinji not came through. And they would've had to do it before Big Z came into the command center or else everyone dies and nobody is left to activate it. That they didn't even mention that option tells me that they don't have a manual detonation.

[]:             Quote Post by monitoradiation » Sun Sep 29, 2013 8:44 pm Kendrix wrote: Uh, this is explained. Big Z took Zerogoki's identification code, and then proceeded to * thrash the computer room *. You know, with these big main computers that control everything in the geofront, including the self-destruct?

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Mon Sep 30, 2013 3:08 am That discussion made me wonder : why Zeruel even absorbed EVA-00 and tried to destroy the Command Center, did he knew that there was a self-destruct mechanism and how to counter it? And if that's the case, then how the hell did he learned that, aren't the Angels supposed to act only by instinct?

[]:             Quote Post by Giji Shinka » Mon Sep 30, 2013 3:23 am ElMariachi wrote: That discussion made me wonder : why Zeruel even absorbed EVA-00 and tried to destroy the Command Center, did he knew that there was a self-destruct mechanism and how to counter it? And if that's the case, then how the hell did he learned that, aren't the Angels supposed to act only by instinct?

[]:             Quote Post by airman4 » Mon Sep 30, 2013 3:34 am ElMariachi wrote: That discussion made me wonder : why Zeruel even absorbed EVA-00 and tried to destroy the Command Center, did he knew that there was a self-destruct mechanism and how to counter it? And if that's the case, then how the hell did he learned that, aren't the Angels supposed to act only by instinct?

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Mon Sep 30, 2013 3:53 am Well, we know from NGE that they are more clever than simple animals and can learn on a limited way, like when Sachiel started to shoot down the observations VTOLs to blind the military, but here that's something impossible to learn through deduction, and even if he suspected that a self-destruct mechanism could exist, how did he knew that he could counter it by absorbing EVA-00, or was it all a mer coincidence? But then why did he ate EVA-00 and not EVA-02 when it KO'd it? Had he just gone through the Command Center to try to kill everyone in it, I could have chalked it up as being a callback to NGE where he did the same thing(he probably felt a large concentration of Lilin nearby while descending and decided to go kill them by instinct), but in combination with absorbing EVA-00 it really looks like he had a precise strategy to counter every plans the Lilin could have to stop him from reaching Lilith.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Mon Sep 30, 2013 4:41 am ElMariachi wrote: But then why did he ate EVA-00 and not EVA-02 when it KO'd it?

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Mon Sep 30, 2013 4:57 am pwhodges wrote: Awareness of Rei's presence and all that that implies?

[]:             Quote Post by monitoradiation » Mon Sep 30, 2013 12:12 pm ElMariachi wrote: That discussion made me wonder : why Zeruel even absorbed EVA-00 and tried to destroy the Command Center, did he knew that there was a self-destruct mechanism and how to counter it? And if that's the case, then how the hell did he learned that, aren't the Angels supposed to act only by instinct?

[]:             Quote Post by wiser3754 » Thu Oct 03, 2013 2:37 am Has everyone forgotten that SEELE converses and plans with the 1st Angel Kaworu? I wouldn't be surprised that the Angels change their form and powers (they could be in Man's image first you know) once they awaken from their coffins given the information that Kaworu receives from SEELE to plan the best method of attack and infiltration to Terminal Dogma. So when big Z comes crashing through the Command Centre his itinerary was to knock out automated defences and self destruct protocols. God, that's so far fetched. One last side note *looking around for Monk Ed* could Terminal Dogma also imply the term "Terminal Illness", meaning death is now a certainty for which there is no alternative to the being who rests in there. . . . . no, I thought not.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Oct 03, 2013 3:34 am Since SEELE had a covenant with Lilith to destroy all the Angel and that Kaworu was aware and collaborating with it(see his discussion with SEELE-01 in the Moon at the end of 1.0), I doubt that they were giving instructions to the Angels to more easily reach Lilith, if they could really do that they would give the Angel false information to made their task harder and the job easier for NERV. As for Terminal Dogma, remember that in Rebuild the terms are inverted : Terminal Dogma is the upper floors of NERV HQ(Hyuuga said that Zeruel infiltrated Terminal Dogma just after blowing up the door of the main shaft), and Central Dogma is the bottommost part of the HQ, where Lilith resides. Besides IIRC Terminal and Central Dogma are a reference to the Bible.
